title: Analysis: West Bloomfield terror attack highlights Hezbollah connections within Shiite Lebanese community in US
description:
Last week, Ayman Mohamad Ghazali, a Lebanese-US dual national, rammed his car into the entrance of Temple Israel, a synagogue in West Bloomfield, Indiana. Ghazali had familial connections to Hezbollah, and evidence shows multiple connections to the US-designated terrorist group among Shiite Lebanese expatriates, including open support for Hezbollah by some individuals within these communities.

description: A top NATO official on Wednesday urged allied countries to rethink how they ensure their defence. Pierre Vandier told a security and defence forum in Paris that many countries have yet to fully grasp the scale of the challenge, particularly when it comes to boosting weapons production, adding that it was not a question of money but of speed.

title: Ex-EU border chief faces French investigation for crimes against humanity
description: The Paris Court of Appeal opened an investigation into the former head of the EU Border agency on March 24 for potentially participating in crimes against humanity. The former border chief, Fabrice Leggeri resigned in 2022 following allegations of mistreatment of migrants at EU borders. Now a lawmaker for France's far-right National Rally party, he denies the allegations.

title: Heritage sites in the Middle East under threat due to conflict
description: The Middle East war is having an impact on the region's heritage sites. Archaeological and Unesco heritage sites have been collateral damage in the war, on all sides. Unesco believes at least 6 heritage sites in the Middle East have been damaged, the Tyre site in Lebanon, four in Iran and the White City in Israel. In Iran, the historic city of isfahan and the Golestan palace in Tehran were also damaged.

title: Iran 'dismisses' US 15-point peace plan, issues its own ceasefire proposal
description: Iran on March 25 an American plan to pause the war in the Middle East, issuing its own counterproposal instead, as it launched more attacks on Israel and Gulf Arab countries. Its own plan includes a halt to killings of its officials, means to make sure no other war is waged against it, reparations for the war, the end of hostilities and Iran’s “exercise of sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.” FRANCE 24's Philip Turle takes a look.

title: Emblematic MeToo trial of Islamic scholar Tariq Ramadan awaits verdict
description: Tariq Ramadan, a well-known Swiss-Egyptian Islamic scholar, has been tried in absentia for the rape of three women in Paris, with the verdict to fall on March 25. The trial is one of the most emblematic of the MeToo movement in France. He has already been convicted in Switzerland, where he is currently hospitalised for multiple sclerosis - which his medical team judged made him unable to attend his trial in France. FRANCE 24's Angela Diffley look

title: Ukrainian drones crash in Estonia and Latvia, raising air defence concerns
description: Stray Ukrainian drones hit Estonia and Latvia early on March 25, one of them slamming into a chimney at a power station. There were no reports of injuries in the incidents, and the drones were believed to be part of a wider Ukrainian attack on Russia targeting energy infrastructure. Latvia says these drone incursions could keep happening in the Baltic region.

title: Generation Jihad | The regime falls when the people rise

description: Ghana and the European Union on March 25 signed their first formal defence partnership. It has also signed a similar agreement with Iceland and Australia. It comes as Ghana tries to prevent a spillover of increased attacks from groups backed by Al-Qaeda and the Islamic State in the region. The unfolding conflict reveals a profound shift in Gulf strategic thinking, Professor Lindstaedt argues, driven less by ideological ambition than by accumulated insecurity. He examines the evolving dynamics between Washington and Tel Aviv in the context of the war on Iran, alongside Israel’s expanding military posture in Lebanon. title: US demands of 15-point peace plan likely 'unacceptable' for Iran

There are some indications: sanction relief, a rollback of Iran's nuclear programme, the monitoring by the IAEA of that programme, shipping through the Strait of Hormuz and an end to support for proxies. FRANCE 24's Philip Turle takes a look at the latest developments.

This record attack had hit Lviv's historic centre and residential houses in the west of the country.

title: Inside the Palestinian camp in Beirut where residents are staying put, for now
description: A FRANCE 24 team visited the Palestinian camp of Bourj el-Barajneh in the heart of Beirut’s southern suburbs, which are frequently struck by Israeli airstrikes. Yet here, many people say they have little choice but to stay. Bourj el-Barajneh was established in 1948 after Palestinians were forced to leave their homes in what became known as the Nakba (‘catastrophe’). title: Russia and Ukraine escalate drone attacks as Moscow starts spring offensive

Russia's air defences also downed nearly 400 incoming Ukrainian drones, making it the largest reported overnight attack on Russian regions and Crimea since the start of the invasion. title: In Lebanon, some schools choose to stay open despite the war
description: In Lebanon, despite ongoing strikes and insecurity, some schools have chosen to remain open. In Beirut, a school located near the southern suburbs regularly targeted continues to welcome students, while others attend classes remotely. How do you teach and learn amidst a war?  FRANCE 24's Serge Berberi and Renée Davis went to meet parents and students - and found some answers.

title: US ramps up military presence in the Middle East amid reported talks

There are plans to send soldiers from the 82nd Airborne Division to support operations against Iran, according to US media reports. They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the region by March 25.

title: War in the Middle East: An opportunity for Iran's minorities?
description: Shiite Persians make up 51 percent of Iran's population. Yet communities like the Azeris, Kurds, Balochs and Arabs also number in the millions. Historically, they have often been ignored or mistreated by the regime in Tehran, both under the shah and the Islamic Republic. These ethnic minorities could play a role in the current conflict raging in the Middle East. FRANCE 24's David Gilbert and Florent Marchais report.

description: Moscow’s forces are intensifying their attacks in southern Ukraine after Kyiv made rare gains along the front.

title: Exclusive: Newsom calls Musk "one of the great disappointments" of this era
description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom praised Elon Musk as this generation's Thomas Edison — then accused the Tesla CEO of surrendering the electric vehicle market to China."It breaks my heart," Newsom said on a new episode of "The Axios Show," describing Musk as "one of the great disappointments" of our time.Why it matters: China already holds 70% of the global EV market. title: Girl, Interrupted: Afghan girls and women shut out of education, careers, and public life
description: Eve Irvine is pleased to welcome Mursal Sayas, Radio Producer & Presenter at Radio Afghanistan International and Public Speaker for Women's Rights. Education, once a pathway toward individual and collective development, has been severed for millions of Afghan girls, transforming years of life into empty duration. This exclusion extends beyond schooling into the erasure of women from public, professional, and even expressive existence. According t

title: For Australia’s Farmers, Fuel Crisis Comes at ‘Worst Possible Time’
description: Their troubles could cascade across Asia because they export a majority of their products.

title: Is Iran’s negotiating position stronger than when US-Israeli war started?
description: Tehran believes so, and will seek to extract concessions that the US and Gulf nations cant agree to, analysts caution.

title: Dreams of survival: How war has restructured Gaza’s job market
description: Gazas professionals adapt careers to survive, shifting from arts and business to whatever they can find to make money.

title: From Pakistan to Egypt, Iran war drives up prices in Global South
description: Developing economies in Asia, Africa and the Middle East are most exposed to surging energy costs.

title: Титов заявил, что глобальная инициатива развития Китая может стать ориентиром для формирования повестки устойчивого разв
description: Модель Китая является ярким примером предсказуемого устойчивого развития и может стать ориентиром его повестки после 2030 года, заявил специальный представитель президента России по связям с международными организациями для достижения целей устойчивого развития Борис Титов."В рамках Глобальной инициативы развития он не просто помогает другим, а создает для них возможности для самостоятельного роста, распространяя такие инструменты развития, как ц

description:

title: Russia, Ukraine tit-for-tat attacks knock out power for over half a million
description: Some 450,000 people without electricity in Belgorod region, while power cut off for 150,000 consumers ‌in ​Chernihiv.

title: US talking to itself, says Iran as Trump claims wheels of diplomacy turning
description: Iranian officials continue to deny any peace talks, amid reports the US sent the country a ceasefire proposal.

title: Iran’s new security boss Mohammad Zolghadr: Why his appointment matters
description: Zolghadr, an ex-IRGC commander, will manage Irans security amid external US-Israeli pressures and internal unrest.

title: NYT: Пентагон направил 2 тыс. десантников на Ближний Восток
description: Пентагон приказал около 2 тыс. военнослужащих 82-й воздушно-десантной дивизии начать переброску на Ближний Восток. Об этом сообщила газета The New York Times со ссылкой на два источника в американском военном министерстве.

title: Facing violence on all fronts, Arab Israelis are unprecedentedly open to mental health help
description:

title: Police chief who responded to Detroit synagogue attack targeted by online vitriol
description:
Man who posted antisemitic meme of the non-Jewish sheriff arrested on unrelated charge, prompting far-right activists to claim censorship, blame Jews for 'murdering free speech'

title: US proposes 15-point plan as Iran opens Strait of Hormuz to 'non-hostile' oil vessels
description: US President Donald Trump said Tuesday he had sent a peace plan to Iran, expressing cautious optimism about ending nearly a month of war. In a potential sign of de-escalation, Tehran announced it would allow “non-hostile” vessels to pass through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz, a key artery for global oil supplies.

title: 'The humanitarian crisis here is worsening by the minute': Israel continues to bomb Lebanon
description: More than a million people have been driven from their homes in Lebanon as Israel continues its bombardment of the country and seizes land south of the Litani River. FRANCE 24 correspondent Renée Davis reports from Beirut on the rising resentment towards Israel's ongoing air strikes.

title: US-Israel war on Iran: What’s happening on day 26 of attacks?
description: Iran, Israel, the Gulf faced new missiles and drones. The US has signalled military escalation and openness to a deal.

description: Developing economies in Asia, Africa and the Middle East are most exposed to surging energy costs.

title: Afghanistan Frees American Dennis Coyle from Detention
description: Dennis Walter Coyle, a researcher from Colorado, had been held since last year by the Taliban government.
